name: Adaptive Compute in Latent World Models: When Depth Helps, Hurts, or Doesn't Matter
summary: arXiv:2607.10203v3 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: Adaptive compute for world models -- early-exit or mixture-of-depths predictors that spend variable depth per rollout step -- presumes that...
description: arXiv:2607.10203v3 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: Adaptive compute for world models -- early-exit or mixture-of-depths predictors that spend variable depth per rollout step -- presumes that extra depth buys better predictions. In autoregressive rollouts, where planning actually happens, that premise requires depth's per-step precision to survive composition. We test it directly with one...
author: Achyuthan Sivasankar
